---
title: "Lanczos"
generated: true
description: "Lanczos windowed-sinc interpolation of order `order` (radius `a`)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry.grid`](../nitrix-geometry-grid.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.geometry._interpolate`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Lanczos -->
```python
class Lanczos
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:Lanczos -->
Lanczos windowed-sinc interpolation of order `order` (radius `a`).

A separable `2a`-tap kernel
`L_a(x) = sinc(x) sinc(x / a)` (`a = order`), the high-fidelity
resampler of the ANTs `LanczosWindowedSinc` class.  The `2a` taps
per axis are `floor(coord) - a + 1 ... floor(coord) + a`; the
per-axis weights are **renormalised to sum to 1**, so the separable
outer product is a partition of unity and constants are preserved
exactly (raw Lanczos weights sum to ~1 but not exactly).

Smooth in both the image values and the sample coordinates, so it is
differentiable end-to-end -- the high-quality differentiable
alternative to [`Linear`](../nitrix-geometry-grid/Linear.qmd) for registration and anti-aliased
resampling.  Unlike [`Linear`](../nitrix-geometry-grid/Linear.qmd) / [`NearestNeighbour`](../nitrix-geometry-grid/NearestNeighbour.qmd) it
has no `map_coordinates` equivalent, so it always uses the explicit
separable gather (no platform branch).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Lanczos.sample -->
```python
sample(image: Float[Array, '*spatial c'], coords: Float[Array, '*out_spatial ndim'], *, mode: BoundaryMode, cval: float) -> Float[Array, '*out_spatial c']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Lanczos.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(order: int = 3) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:Lanczos -->
::: {.callout-note}
This is the ANTs *algorithm class*, not bit-exact ITK parity (ITK's
windowed-sinc radius / normalisation conventions differ in detail).
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:Lanczos -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
| `c` | feature channels (channels-last) |
| `*out_spatial` | the output grid's spatial axes (variadic) |
| `ndim` | number of spatial dimensions |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
